purpose: Carry-forward from 2026-04-26_kick_first_real_bench. Goal: invoke kick_bench.hexa live (no --dry-run) across both installed backends (claude_code + codex_cli) with the haiku topic, to close F33/F34 multi-backend status. Outcome: BLOCKED at the live-spawn precondition gate — kick_dispatch.hexa rejects with reason=no-parent-sid for both backends. F33/F34 remain OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED, but with a sharper, mechanical root cause (HIVE_PARENT_SID unset in the worker environment) and a hexa-only resolution path (a parent-SID provider helper, or honoring an opt-in env override).

obstruction.step_1_identification: kick_dispatch.hexa lines 877-882 enforce parent_sid.len() == 0 → exit(2) with reason=no-parent-sid before any subagent_dispatch.hexa invocation. parent_sid is sourced from env("HIVE_PARENT_SID") at kick_bench.hexa:371 and forwarded only if non-empty. Background hive workers (this exact one) do NOT have HIVE_PARENT_SID set.
obstruction.step_2_documentation: OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED with verbatim reject line captured above. Both backends fail identically — the gate is environmental, not backend-specific. Codex_cli and claude_code adapters are intact; the precondition simply rejects before reaching them.
obstruction.step_3_structural_reframe:
The gate is correct policy (raw 44 contract requires parent SID for subagent dispatch). The honest closure path is NOT to bypass the gate but to make parent SID acquisition possible inside an unattended worker context. Three hexa-only options:
  (A) parent-sid provider helper: tool/hive_parent_sid.hexa that mints (or fetches from a hive control plane) a usable SID then exports to env. Caller wraps: HIVE_PARENT_SID=$(hexa run tool/hive_parent_sid.hexa) hexa run tool/kick_bench.hexa ...
  (B) honor a SID from a sentinel file the orchestrator itself drops at start (e.g., state/kick/parent_sid.txt) when running outside a hive session. kick_dispatch.hexa would prefer env, fall back to file, fail-loud if both missing.
  (C) make the bench orchestrator (kick_bench.hexa) itself the parent — register its own SID with hive at startup and forward it to children. This is the cleanest semantically (bench IS the parent); requires a hexa-only "register-as-parent" entrypoint in tool/subagent_dispatch.hexa or a sibling.
  Recommended: (C) for long-term, (A) as a near-term hexa-only shim. None of the three modify atlas.n6 / signals.n6 / millennium.n6 or introduce non-hexa source files.
obstruction.step_4_sanity_check: Code path verified by Read: kick_dispatch.hexa:878 (`if parent_sid.len() == 0`), kick_bench.hexa:371 (`let mut parent_sid = env("HIVE_PARENT_SID")`), kick_bench.hexa:449 (`if parent_sid.len() > 0 { cmd = cmd + " --parent-sid " + _shell_quote(parent_sid) }`). Path is structurally clean — single source of truth for parent SID, single gate, single reject reason. The reject is doing its job.
obstruction.step_5_cumulative_learning: The 'OBSTRUCTION_DOCUMENTED' verdict from yesterday's cycle implicitly assumed the live orchestrator could run in a fresh worker context. It cannot, by current contract. Future bench cycles MUST either (a) run inside an interactive hive session that already has HIVE_PARENT_SID set, or (b) ship one of the three resolutions above. Until then, ANY worker-context bench run will hit this same gate. This is now a known, mechanical, documented blocker rather than an unknown.
